campbell.title: Campbell City Council — September 2, 2025
campbell.summary: The Campbell City Council met to review the city's financial health for July 2025, including how the city's investments performed and spending reports from the treasurer. The council also discussed plans to improve transportation options across Campbell, looking at how to better connect different ways of getting around the city.
keyTopics: July 2025 investment performance and money management
City spending and budget report for July
New multimodal transportation plan (buses, bikes, cars, walking)

saratoga.title: Saratoga City Council — March 18, 2026
saratoga.summary: The city council held a public hearing on March 18, 2026, where residents could comment on a proposed new rule or policy. After hearing public input, the council moved forward by introducing an ordinance (a new city law) and skipping the usual first reading requirement to speed up the process. The council directed staff to add this ordinance to the next meeting's agenda for final approval.
keyTopics: Public hearing held for community input on proposed ordinance
First reading of ordinance waived to expedite approval
Ordinance scheduled for adoption at next regular council meeting
Staff directed to place measure on consent calendar

los-gatos.title: Los Gatos City Council — April 7, 2026
los-gatos.summary: The Los Gatos City Council met on April 7, 2026, to approve meeting minutes from several March sessions and authorize the Town Manager to sign a construction contract with QLM, Inc. The meeting primarily handled administrative housekeeping and project approval, though the specific details of the construction project weren't fully visible in the agenda summary.
keyTopics: Approval of multiple March 2026 meeting minutes
Construction contract authorization with QLM, Inc.
Town Manager contract execution authority

san-jose.title: San José City Council — April 9, 2026
san-jose.summary: The San José City Council met to review how well the city is serving its children and young people, including updates on youth programs, summer safety initiatives, and youth mentoring services. They also heard reports on code enforcement activities and discussed plans to improve the city's library buildings and services.
keyTopics: Youth Empowerment Alliance and mentoring programs for young people
Safe Summer Initiative to keep kids engaged during school break
Youth Intervention Services to help at-risk young people
Code Enforcement activities and violations citywide
Library facility upgrades and customer service improvements

mountain-view.title: Mountain View City Council — April 7, 2026
mountain-view.summary: The Mountain View City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026, that included both in-person and online participation options. Residents were able to join the discussion virtually via Zoom or phone to watch proceedings and provide public comments on agenda items.
keyTopics: Virtual meeting access via Zoom and phone dial-in
Public comment procedures for remote participants
Hybrid meeting format (in-person and online combined)

sunnyvale.title: Sunnyvale City Council — April 9, 2026
sunnyvale.summary: The Sunnyvale Human Relations Commission held a meeting on April 9, 2026, where they honored the flag and heard personal stories from three commissioners about their experiences in Sunnyvale. The group also approved the official minutes from their previous meeting in March.
keyTopics: Personal stories shared by Commissioners 5, 6, and 7 about their connections to Sunnyvale
Approval of March 12, 2026 meeting minutes
Human Relations Commission regular business and operations

cupertino.title: Cupertino City Council — April 8, 2026
cupertino.summary: The Cupertino City Council met on April 8, 2026 to discuss several community initiatives including a food program called Budget and Bites, a youth-led organization called Unmuted Tabs, and summer job opportunities for young people in the city. The council also approved minutes from their previous meeting and opened the floor for residents to speak about any local issues for up to 3 minutes each.
keyTopics: Budget and Bites food program presentation
Unmuted Tabs youth-led organization
City summer jobs for young residents
Public comment period for residents

santa-clara.title: Santa Clara City Council — April 7, 2026
santa-clara.summary: The Santa Clara City Council held a meeting on April 7, 2026 using a hybrid format, meaning residents could attend in person or join remotely via Zoom. The city is providing multiple ways for the public to participate and submit comments about agenda items.
keyTopics: Hybrid meeting format (in-person and Zoom options available)
Remote participation via Zoom with meeting ID 997-0675-9306
Phone dial-in option: 1(669) 900-6833
Written public comments can be submitted through eComment tab on City Council Agenda page

milpitas.title: Milpitas City Council — April 7, 2026
milpitas.summary: The Milpitas City Council met to review and approve several administrative matters, including updates to the city's federal funding plan for 2025-2030 and new work requests from individual council members. The council also discussed changes to how council vacancies are filled and approved meeting minutes and calendars.
keyTopics: Update to federal funding plan (Consolidated Plan) for affordable housing and community development
Changes to the council vacancy process
New work requests from council members being reviewed
Approval of meeting minutes and upcoming meeting schedule

palo-alto.title: Palo Alto City Council — March 31, 2026
palo-alto.summary: The Palo Alto City Council reviewed and approved the city's gas utility financial plan for the next fiscal year, including changes to how much residents and businesses will pay for natural gas service. The council adjusted gas rates for three different customer categories—residential, smaller commercial, and large commercial users—based on the utility's financial needs and forecasts.
keyTopics: Gas rate increases for residential customers (Schedule G-1)
Rate adjustments for commercial and master-metered properties (Schedule G-2)
Large commercial gas service rate changes (Schedule G-3)
Transfer of utility reserves and general fund allocations
FY 2027 gas utility financial forecast approval
